panels.title: Cluster CPU Efficiency
panels.description: Actual CPU used vs total allocatable. Low = paying for idle nodes.

panels.title: Cluster Memory Efficiency
panels.description: Actual memory used vs total allocatable. Low = paying for idle memory.

panels.title: CPU Request Commitment
panels.description: CPU requested vs allocatable. High = nodes are fully committed (may block scheduling).

panels.title: Memory Request Commitment
panels.description: Memory requested vs allocatable. High = nodes are fully committed.

panels.title: CPU Waste Ratio
panels.description: Requested but unused CPU. High = pods are over-provisioned.

panels.title: Memory Waste Ratio
panels.description: Requested but unused memory. High = pods are over-provisioned.

panels.title: Idle Nodes (CPU < 10%)
panels.description: Nodes where CPU usage is below 10% — candidates for scale-down.

panels.title: Pods With No CPU Usage
panels.description: Pods consuming 0 CPU — zombie/idle workloads wasting resources.

panels.title: Node CPU Efficiency % (Actual vs Allocatable)
panels.description: Actual CPU usage % per node. Below 30% = underutilized, candidate for scale-down.

panels.title: Node Memory Efficiency % (Actual vs Allocatable)
panels.description: How much of each node's memory is actually being used. Below 30% = underutilized node.

panels.title: Node CPU — Reserved vs Actual vs Allocatable
panels.description: 3-way comparison: what is available, what is requested, what is actually used.

panels.title: Node Memory — Reserved vs Actual vs Allocatable
panels.description: 3-way comparison: what is available, what is requested, what is actually used.

panels.title: CPU Usage by Namespace
panels.description: Which namespaces are consuming the most CPU over time.

panels.title: Memory Usage by Namespace
panels.description: Which namespaces are consuming the most memory over time.

panels.title: CPU Requested by Namespace
panels.description: CPU reserved/committed per namespace — what the scheduler sees.

panels.title: Memory Requested by Namespace
panels.description: Memory reserved/committed per namespace — what the scheduler sees.

panels.title: Namespace Resource Summary
panels.description: CPU and memory — requested vs actual per namespace. Large gap = over-provisioned namespace.

panels.title: Top CPU Wasters — Request vs Actual
panels.description: Pods with largest gap between CPU requested and CPU used. Right-size these first.

panels.title: Top Memory Wasters — Request vs Actual
panels.description: Pods with largest gap between memory requested and memory used. Right-size these first.

panels.title: 🏆 Top Over-Provisioned Pods — Rightsizing Candidates
panels.description: Pods sorted by CPU waste (request - actual). These are your biggest cost saving opportunities.

panels.title: Pods With Near-Zero CPU Usage
panels.description: Pods consuming less than 1m CPU — likely idle or zombie workloads.

panels.title: CPU Throttling % by Pod
panels.description: Pods being heavily throttled — CPU limit is too low, causing slowness.

targets.expr: sum(rate(container_cpu_cfs_throttled_seconds_total{container!="", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}[5m])) by (namespace, pod) / sum(rate(container_cpu_cfs_periods_total{container!="", namespace=~"$namespace", pod=~"$pod"}[5m])) by (namespace, pod) * 100

panels.title: Zombie Pods — Zero CPU Usage (last 15m)
panels.description: Pods with no CPU activity. Candidates for scale-down to 0 or removal.

panels.title: Unused PVCs — Not Mounted to Any Running Pod
panels.description: PVCs provisioned but not attached to any pod. You are paying for unused storage.

panels.title: HPA Utilization % (Current / Max Replicas)
panels.description: How close each HPA is to its max. Near 100% = autoscaler at capacity, may need higher max.

panels.title: HPA Current vs Min vs Max Replicas
panels.description: Replicas sitting at min = idle capacity. Replicas at max = needs scaling up.

panels.title: Replica Count Over Time by Deployment
panels.description: Track scaling events — sudden spikes may indicate inefficient scaling policies.
